steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank fills "useful as [blank] cultural change, revealing shifts in norms, values, and concerns." The rest of the sentence says these objects reveal cultural shifts — so the blank must mean something like "indicators of" or "signs of." The word or phrase must also fit grammatically as a noun phrase followed by "cultural change."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'alternatives to': Means substitutes or replacements. The objects aren't replacements for cultural change; they reveal it. ✗
  • Choice B — 'conjectures about': Means guesses or speculations. The objects themselves aren't guesses; they are evidence that reveals real shifts. ✗
  • Choice C — 'indices of': "Indices" means indicators or signs. The objects function as signs pointing to cultural change — exactly what "revealing shifts" confirms. ✓
  • Choice D — 'pretexts for': Means false excuses or justifications. The objects aren't being used as cover for something else; they genuinely reveal change. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
